title: France squad news for FIFA World Cup 2026: Deschamps provides Saliba injury update
original_text: France squad news for FIFA World Cup 2026: Deschamps provides Saliba injury update left Saliba joined the rest of his France teammates on Tuesday at their pre-tournament camp outside of Paris after playing for Arsenal in its UEFA Champions League final loss to Paris Saint-Germain at the weekend. AFP https://ss-i.thgim.com/public/incoming/ojnh5d/article71058327.ece/alternates/FREE_1200/AFP_B4WE92U.jpg FRANCK FIFE (From L) French national football team forward Bradley Barcola, defender Willliam Saliba, forward Kylian Mbappe, midfielder Rayan Cherki, head coach Didier Deschamps and forward Desire Doue attend a training session on the eve of the friendly football match between France and Ivory-Coast ahead of the FIFA 2026 World Cup, in Nantes on June 3, 2026. (Photo by FRANCK FIFE / AFP) Deschamps, who will step down as coach after the World Cup, said he would rotate his squad for the upcoming friendlies, with Les Bleus also due to play Northern Ireland in Lille next Monday. Nantes France coach Didier Deschamps said on Wednesday that defender William Saliba is “fine” despite reports he was a doubt for the FIFA World Cup 2026 with a back injury. Saliba joined the rest of his France teammates on Tuesday at their pre-tournament camp outside of Paris after playing for Arsenal in its UEFA Champions League final loss to Paris Saint-Germain at the weekend. France will face Ivory Coast in Nantes in a World Cup warm-up match on Thursday, but Deschamps is expected to limit Saliba’s workload as a precaution. “William is fine and will be managed. If he had needed to play tomorrow, he would have played,” Deschamps said. Deschamps, who will step down as coach after the World Cup, said he would rotate his squad for the upcoming friendlies, with Les Bleus also due to play Northern Ireland in Lille next Monday. Ousmane Dembele and Desire Doue are among five PSG players in the squad who will be carefully monitored by Deschamps following Saturday’s triumph in Budapest. “I will manage the six players involved in the Champions League, even more so those who started, given what lies ahead,” Deschamps said.
